name: hsl-language-support
description: Base language support for Hamilton Standard Language (HSL)
author: Zachary Milot

program.description: Absolute path to the HSL method file to run.

hamiltonDir.description: Path to the Hamilton installation directory.

internalConsoleOptions.description: Controls when the Debug Console panel is opened.

configurationSnippets.label: HSL: Run Method
configurationSnippets.description: Run an HSL method file using the HSL simulation debugger.

github.copilot.chat.codeGeneration.instr.text:
HSL (Hamilton Standard Language) is a C-like language for Hamilton liquid handling robots. It has critical differences from C/C++/JavaScript that MUST be followed:

1. VARIABLE DECLARATIONS MUST BE AT THE TOP OF EVERY CODE BLOCK: All variable declarations must appear BEFORE any executable statement in the same { } block. This includes function bodies, method bodies, namespace bodies, struct bodies, nested blocks, and file scope. Declaring a variable after any executable line in the same block is a COMPILE ERROR. Nested { } blocks reset the rule -- declarations can appear at the top of nested blocks.

2. 'continue' IS NOT A VALID KEYWORD: HSL does not support 'continue' in loops. Use if/else restructuring instead. For example, instead of 'if (cond) { continue; }' use 'if (!cond) { /* body */ }'.

3. NO COMPOUND ASSIGNMENT OPERATORS: HSL does not have +=, -=, *=, /= operators. Use expanded form: 'x = x + 1;' instead of 'x += 1;'. Also '=+' and '=-' are not valid -- use '= +' or '= -' (with a space).

4. FUNCTIONS REQUIRE BOTH A DECLARATION AND A DEFINITION: Every function needs a forward declaration (prototype) AND a definition (implementation). Missing either is an error. Duplicate definitions are also errors.

5. DATA TYPES: variable (general-purpose), string, float, short, long, char, object, sequence, device, resource, dialog, timer, event, file.

6. MODIFIERS: private, static, const, global, synchronized.

7. FUNCTION SYNTAX: 'function Name(variable param) returnType { }' -- return type is required (use 'void' for no return). Methods use: 'method main() { }'.

8. STRING CONCATENATION: Use '+' operator or StrConcat() function.

9. ERROR HANDLING: Use 'onerror goto Label;' pattern with labeled error handler blocks. Use err.GetDescription(), err.Clear(), abort.

10. CONTROL FLOW: if, else, for, while, do, loop, switch, case, default, break, return, onerror, goto, abort, throw, try, catch, fork, join, lock, unlock. NOT supported: continue.

11. NO C/C++/JS IDIOMS: Do not use 'for (int i = 0; ...)' -- declare 'variable i;' at block top, then 'for (i = 0; ...)'.
